question: Abstract FDV above $2B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will South Korea GDP growth in Q1 2026 be less than 0%?
description:
This market will resolve according to South Korea's real Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth rate (Year-on-Year, %) in the first quarter of 2026, as reported in the Bank of Korea’s Real Gross Domestic Product (Advance Estimate) release for the first quarter of 2026, scheduled for release on April 23, 2026.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.bok.or.kr/eng/singl/newsDataEng/list.do?pageIndex=&targetDepth=3&menuNo=400423&syncMenuChekKey=1&searchCnd=1&searchKwd=&depth2=400417&depth3=400423&date=&sdate=&edate=&sort=1&pageUnit=10

The relevant figure may be found in the table titled “Growth Rates by Type of Economic Activity and Component of Expenditure (original series, percentage change over previous year)” in the row “Gross Domestic Product” and the column for the specified quarter. Changes in South Korea’s GDP reporting format will not disqualify a published figure from counting.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports year-on-year GDP growth rates to only one decimal point (e.g. 0.3%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.bok.or.kr/eng/stats/statsPublictSchdul/listCldr.do?menuNo=400359&date=2026-01

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on South Korea's Q1 2026 GDP growth remains tightly contested, with market-implied odds favoring 2.5%+ at 35% narrowly ahead of 1.5-1.9% and 0.5-0.9% bands around 33% and 32.5%, reflecting deep uncertainty from President Yoon Suk-yeol's December 2024 impeachment amid a failed martial law bid. Recent Q3 GDP expanded just 0.1% quarter-over-quarter, hampered by slumping construction and soft exports to China, while Bank of Korea forecasts peg 2025 growth at 1.9%—vulnerable to U.S. tariff risks under Trump and policy paralysis. Upside hinges on semiconductor recovery and impeachment resolution by early 2025; downside risks from prolonged instability elevate <0% odds to 31%, underscoring traders' bets on volatile chaebol-driven dynamics.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.bok.or.kr/eng/singl/newsDataEng/list.do?pageIndex=&targetDepth=3&menuNo=400423&syncMenuChekKey=1&searchCnd=1&searchKwd=&depth2=400417&depth3=400423&date=&sdate=&edate=&sort=1&pageUnit=10

The relevant figure may be found in the table titled “Growth Rates by Type of Economic Activity and Component of Expenditure (original series, percentage change over previous year)” in the row “Gross Domestic Product” and the column for the specified quarter. Changes in South Korea’s GDP reporting format will not disqualify a published figure from counting.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports year-on-year GDP growth rates to only one decimal point (e.g. 0.3%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.bok.or.kr/eng/stats/statsPublictSchdul/listCldr.do?menuNo=400359&date=2026-01

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on South Korea's Q1 2026 GDP growth remains tightly contested, with market-implied odds favoring 2.5%+ at 35% narrowly ahead of 1.5-1.9% and 0.5-0.9% bands around 33% and 32.5%, reflecting deep uncertainty from President Yoon Suk-yeol's December 2024 impeachment amid a failed martial law bid. Recent Q3 GDP expanded just 0.1% quarter-over-quarter, hampered by slumping construction and soft exports to China, while Bank of Korea forecasts peg 2025 growth at 1.9%—vulnerable to U.S. tariff risks under Trump and policy paralysis. Upside hinges on semiconductor recovery and impeachment resolution by early 2025; downside risks from prolonged instability elevate <0% odds to 31%, underscoring traders' bets on volatile chaebol-driven dynamics.

question: Will the Bank of Brazil increase the Selic rate after April 2026 meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the Selic rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Brazil's April 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Brazil after its April 2026 policy meeting, currently scheduled for April 27-28, as listed on the official Bank of Brazil calendar: https://www.bcb.gov.br/en/about/bcb-calendar

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Brazil's statement for their March meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Selic rate decrease at 72.5% implied probability for the Banco Central do Brasil's April 16-17 Copom meeting, driven by sustained disinflation and economic softening. March IPCA inflation printed at 0.16% month-over-month and 3.93% year-over-year—comfortably within the 3% ±1.5% target—bolstered by falling food prices despite sticky services. Industrial output contracted 0.7% in February, unemployment rose to 7.7%, and retail sales weakened, signaling room for easing from 10.75%. No-change odds at 25.5% reflect caution on core inflation persistence, while hikes remain negligible at 1.6%, consistent with global rate-cut cycles. Upcoming GDP data could sway final positioning.

question: Will the Fed Pause–Pause–Pause in the next three decisions (Mar–Apr–Jun)?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds rate. The decisions on the target federal funds rate are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve according to the decisions made by the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings: March 17-18, 2026; April 28-29; and June 16-17.

A qualifying cut occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lower compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying hike occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is higher compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying pause occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is equal to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

If the Fed publishes a different combination than any listed, this market will resolve to "Other". Any rate hike will be encompassed by "Other".

Emergency rate cuts outside the regularly scheduled meetings will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meetings:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an 83.5% implied probability to the Fed maintaining steady rates across its March, May, and June 2025 meetings (Pause–Pause–Pause), reflecting sticky inflation and robust economic data that have cooled aggressive rate-cut bets. December CPI printed at 2.9% year-over-year—above consensus—and January's jobs report added 256,000 nonfarm payrolls with unemployment steady at 4.1%, signaling no recessionary urgency. Fed Chair Powell's hawkish December testimony underscored patience, aligning with the FOMC's median dot-plot projection of just two 25bps cuts in 2025, likely deferred. The 12% odds on Pause–Pause–Cut capture trader hedging for potential softening in upcoming PCE inflation or GDP releases, while "Other" at 4.3% accounts for hikes amid persistent wage pressures.

question: Will Silver (SI) hit (HIGH) $230 by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing a modest implied probability for silver futures (SI) reaching $30 by June 30, reflecting proximity to current spot levels around $29.50 amid cooling inflation and Fed rate cut bets boosting precious metals. Primary drivers include a weakening U.S. dollar index below 105 and surging industrial demand from solar panel production, up 25% YoY per World Silver Survey data. However, hawkish FOMC rhetoric from the June 11-12 meeting and upcoming CPI release on June 12 could cap gains if inflation surprises higher. Historical precedent shows silver often rallies into summer on green energy tailwinds, but trader consensus hedges against renewed dollar strength.

question: Hurupay FDV above $5M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Hurupay's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

question: Will Silver (SI) hit (HIGH) $250 by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing a modest implied probability for silver futures (SI) reaching $30 by June 30, reflecting proximity to current spot levels around $29.50 amid cooling inflation and Fed rate cut bets boosting precious metals. Primary drivers include a weakening U.S. dollar index below 105 and surging industrial demand from solar panel production, up 25% YoY per World Silver Survey data. However, hawkish FOMC rhetoric from the June 11-12 meeting and upcoming CPI release on June 12 could cap gains if inflation surprises higher. Historical precedent shows silver often rallies into summer on green energy tailwinds, but trader consensus hedges against renewed dollar strength.

question: Will OpenAI release a social network in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if OpenAI launches a social network by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A social network refers to a platform or service developed by OpenAI that is primarily intended for users to create profiles or identities and publicly share, view, or interact with content or other users in a feed, network, or similar format. 

Tools or features whose primary purpose is not to function as a social network, even if they allow limited user interaction, such as comments, reactions, or sharing, will not qualify unless they are clearly released and presented by OpenAI as a social network, social platform, or similar.

A qualifying social network must be publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by OpenAI as being accessible to the general public.

Social networks may qualify regardless of whether they are launched as a standalone platform or as social-network functionality integrated into another OpenAI product, provided that OpenAI clearly presents the product or feature as a social network or social platform, or similar.

Messaging or messenger-style applications solely designed for private one-to-one or small-group communication will not qualify for this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from OpenAI; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders heavily favor "No" at 75.5% market-implied probability for OpenAI releasing a social network in 2026, driven primarily by the absence of any official announcements, roadmaps, or prototypes from the company signaling such a pivot. OpenAI's strategic focus remains on frontier AI models like the o1 reasoning series and anticipated GPT-5 iterations, as confirmed in recent developer updates and Sam Altman's public statements emphasizing AGI pursuit over consumer social platforms. Lingering 2024 rumors of social features in ChatGPT—dismissed as unverified speculation—have faded amid competitive pressures from entrenched players like Meta and X, plus regulatory scrutiny on AI deployments. Key catalysts include OpenAI's December 2025 board meetings and potential 2026 model launches, where social ambitions would need explicit confirmation to shift odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors Gold (GC) futures closing above $2,600 per ounce by December 31, with market-implied odds around 75% for the upper price bins, propelled by post-election inflation fears from anticipated Trump tariffs and sustained central bank buying. Spot gold recently touched record highs near $2,750 amid a weakening real yield environment and geopolitical tensions in Ukraine and the Middle East, though a stronger USD (DXY up 2% since Election Day) has capped gains. Key catalysts ahead include November CPI on December 11, the Fed's December 18 FOMC for rate cut signals, and year-end positioning flows; a hawkish dot plot could trigger a pullback toward $2,500 support.

question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $7,000 by end of December?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of December 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

question: OpenAI $1T+ valuation in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI achieves a public valuation of $1 trillion USD or more, or raises a private funding round that values the company at $1 trillion or more between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

To qualify, the valuation must be explicitly confirmed by OpenAI or an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. 

The resolution source will be OpenAI’s official communications, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent funding talks valuing OpenAI at up to $150 billion post-money have propelled the market-implied 53% "Yes" probability for a $1 trillion-plus valuation by 2026, reflecting trader optimism around annualized revenue exceeding $3.7 billion and Microsoft’s deepening integration via Azure infrastructure. However, skepticism persists amid soaring compute costs—projected at tens of billions annually—and regulatory headwinds like potential FTC antitrust scrutiny of Big Tech AI partnerships. Balancing these are competitive pressures from xAI and Anthropic. Decisive catalysts include GPT-5’s expected Q1 2025 debut, Stargate supercomputer progress, and funding round closures; strong demos or investments could surge odds past 70%, while delays might drop them below 40%.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects surging optimism for Anthropic reaching a $500 billion-plus valuation by 2026, driven primarily by its rapid scaling in frontier AI models and strategic hyperscaler partnerships. Claude 3.5 Sonnet's benchmark-leading performance against GPT-4o and Llama 3.1 has solidified Anthropic's competitive edge, while Amazon's $4 billion investment—expandable to $8 billion—and Google's prior $2 billion commitment provide vast AWS compute resources fueling growth. Recent reports of a potential $40 billion funding round underscore investor appetite amid the AI supercycle, with projections hinging on Claude 4's launch and enterprise adoption. Historical parallels to OpenAI's valuation trajectory from $29 billion in 2023 to $157 billion today justify the 84% implied probability, though execution risks like regulatory scrutiny on AI safety loom.

question: Will Canada have the highest unemployment rate since 2016 this year?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (15 years and over, total) reported by Statistics Canada in the Labour Force Survey for any month of 2026 is higher than that of any other month since January 2017 (inclusive). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is the Labor Force Survey, published by Statistics Canada every month at https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/n1/dai-quo/cal1-eng.htm.

Any revisions to the data after the first qualifying release will not count toward this market's resolution; only the initial figure released for each month will qualify. This market will resolve immediately upon a qualifying release of data.

If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 88% implied probability that Canada's 2024 annual unemployment rate will not reach the highest level since 2016, driven by Statistics Canada data showing September's rate at 6.6%—elevated from 2023's 5.8% average amid high interest rates and softening job growth, yet well below the 7.5% in 2021 and 9.5% peak in 2020. Bank of Canada projections and OECD forecasts peg 2024's average around 6.3-6.4%, reflecting expected stabilization post-rate cuts in June and September. Key catalysts include November 1st jobs report and Q4 GDP releases, with traders pricing in no recession deep enough for pre-COVID highs.

question: Will Xi Jinping purge Wang Yi in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if either of the following conditions are met by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

1) The listed individual is removed or resigns from their primary political post or from their position, if any, on the Chinese Politburo or its standing committee, with a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor.

2) The listed individual is expelled from the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). 

Qualifying announcements and reporting of a purge before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal/expulsion goes into effect.

A listed individual leaving office at the end of a regularly scheduled term, or being removed/resigning from office without a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor, will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from the Chinese Government and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on potential Xi Jinping purges in 2026 reflects ongoing CCP anti-corruption drives, with recent investigations of top military figures like Central Military Commission member Miao Hua in December 2024 amplifying speculation of broader elite shakeups. High-profile cases, including former defense ministers Li Shangfu and Wei Fenghe, underscore Xi's consolidation of power amid PLA Rocket Force scandals, prompting bets on Politburo Standing Committee members such as Li Qiang or Cai Qi as next targets. Ahead of the 2027 Party Congress, plenary sessions in 2025-2026 could reveal personnel shifts, though no confirmed plans exist; markets price "no major purge" as a viable outcome given historical patterns of pre-congress stability.

question: Will Xi Jinping purge Li Xi in 2026?
description:

1) The listed individual is removed or resigns from their primary political post or from their position, if any, on the Chinese Politburo or its standing committee, with a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor.

question: Will Hamas agree to disarm by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hamas officially announces it will disarm in the Gaza Strip by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only credible announcements, for example by the widely acknowledged leadership of Hamas, will qualify.

For the purposes of this market, "disarm" refers to a public commitment to relinquish or dismantle its military, whether partially or completely, in the Gaza Strip.

Announcements of partial disarmament (e.g., surrendering a class of weapons or agreeing to disarm in stages or a certain region) will qualify as long as it is part of an acknowledged disarmament process.

Only official announcements will qualify. Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.

Primary resolution sources will include official statements from Hamas leadership; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stalled ceasefire talks between Israel and Hamas drive low trader consensus on Hamas agreeing to disarm by the deadline, as Hamas officials have repeatedly rejected demilitarization demands in recent Qatar- and Egypt-mediated rounds. The latest Doha negotiations in late October collapsed over core disputes, including Israeli troop withdrawal from Gaza, permanent truce terms, and Hamas retaining armed capabilities for "defense." Hamas statements affirm no surrender of weapons, while Israel insists on dismantling militant infrastructure. Upcoming indirect talks, potentially influenced by U.S. diplomatic pressure post-election, represent key catalysts, though historical impasses suggest limited progress absent major concessions.

question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $9,000 by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on gold futures (GC) prices by end-June tilts bullish, with Polymarket odds implying a 65% chance of surpassing $2,400/oz, driven primarily by Federal Reserve rate cut expectations following softer May CPI data showing core inflation at 3.4% year-over-year. Current GC front-month trades near $2,330/oz, up 15% YTD amid a weakening U.S. dollar (DXY at 104.5) and persistent central bank buying, including China's recent 5-tonne addition. Key risks include the June 12 CPI release and FOMC meeting (June 11-12), where dot-plot revisions could strengthen the dollar if cuts are deferred; historical precedent shows gold rallying 5-10% post-pigeonhole Fed signals, though renewed risk-on flows may cap gains.

question: Luigi Mangione out of custody before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Luigi Mangione is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Mangione is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Mangione is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in him leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

If Mangione participates in a successful jailbreak or is otherwise liberated from state custody for any length of time, this market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Mangione to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader conviction behind the 93.5% implied probability for "No" on Luigi Mangione exiting custody before 2027 stems primarily from his December 2024 bail denial amid New York state charges of first-degree murder and terrorism in the UnitedHealthcare CEO killing, bolstered by federal firearms offenses carrying life sentences. Overwhelming forensic evidence—including the ghost gun, silencer, and manifesto—plus his extradition from Pennsylvania solidifies the case, with trial likely delayed into 2026 amid pre-trial motions. Public fascination with Mangione as an anti-corporate folk hero fuels viral discourse but hasn't swayed judicial momentum. Realistic challenges include unforeseen health crises prompting release or a plea deal, though both remain low-probability outliers given prosecutorial resolve and custody safeguards.

question: Will OpenAI announce earbuds or headphones in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to "Yes" if the listed type of consumer hardware product is publicly announced by OpenAI by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The announcement of a qualifying consumer hardware product within the market timeframe will suffice, regardless of whether or when the product is released.

If a device fulfills multiple device categories, then all relevant categories will resolve to "Yes".

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward OpenAI announcing a new frontier large language model in 2026, with implied probabilities around 55-65% for options like "GPT-5 or successor," reflecting the company's consistent annual model cadence from GPT-4o to o1 reasoning series. This sentiment stems from Sam Altman's recent roadmap hints at scaling inference and agentic AI like Operator, amid delays in past releases underscoring execution risks. Competitive pressures from Anthropic's Claude 4 and xAI's Grok-3 intensify focus on compute advantages via Microsoft's Stargate supercomputer. Key watchpoints include OpenAI's fall DevDay 2025 and Q1 2026 funding rounds, which could signal shifts toward hardware or robotics pivots.

question: Will January be the best month for Bitcoin in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the calendar month in 2026 during which Bitcoin has the highest percentage change.

The “Change” value shown for each monthly candle will be used. A monthly candle is considered finalized once the following month’s candle is published.

The resolution source is Binance, using the BTC/USDT trading pair:
https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT

If two or more months are tied for the highest percentage change, this market will resolve to the earliest month chronologically.

Only Binance BTC/USDT data will be used. Prices from other exchanges, trading pairs, or data sources will not be considered.

If it becomes impossible for a given month to have the highest percentage change in 2026, that outcome may resolve to “No” immediately.

question: Tesla and xAI merger officially announced by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that Tesla, Inc. will be, has been, or is being acquired by or merged with xAI, or vice versa, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by Tesla or xAI within this market's timeframe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether or when the announced acquisition/merger actually occurs.

Announcements of partial sales may count, as long as the acquiring company acquires a controlling interest in the other company. A “controlling interest” refers to a change in ownership sufficient to control the company’s strategic decisions (typically more than 50% of equity, or equivalent control via voting and governance rights). Transactions or investments that do not result in a transfer of controlling interest will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Tesla or xAI; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no Tesla-SpaceX merger announcement by June 30, with 90.5% market-implied odds on "No," driven by the stark absence of official statements, leaks, or strategic hints from Elon Musk or company filings. The firms operate in divergent domains—Tesla's electric vehicles and autonomy versus SpaceX's rocketry and Starlink—complicating any union amid antitrust scrutiny and SpaceX's private status. Musk has historically emphasized their independence, redirecting merger speculation toward Tesla-xAI instead. Recent Tesla focus on Robotaxi and Optimus events shows no pivot. A surprise Musk tweet or SEC filing could shift odds, but timelines and valuations make it improbable before quarter-end.

question: Will SpaceX or OpenAI IPO first?
description:
This market will resolve to "SpaceX" if SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) before OpenAI completes an IPO by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. 

This market will resolve to "OpenAI" if OpenAI completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) before SpaceX completes an IPO by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. 

This market will resolve 50-50 if:
- Neither SpaceX nor OpenAI completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET;
- Both SpaceX and OpenAI complete an IPO on the same calendar date (ET); or
- By December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, both companies become unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors SpaceX going public first at 88% implied probability, driven primarily by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's repeated public statements ruling out a near-term IPO, including recent interviews emphasizing the company's focus on AGI development over public markets amid regulatory scrutiny from FTC antitrust probes and AI safety concerns. question: SpaceX or OpenAI higher IPO Market Cap?
description:
This market will resolve to “SpaceX” if the official closing price for SpaceX’s market capitalization on its first trading day is above the official closing price for OpenAI’s market capitalization on its first trading day.

This market will resolve to “OpenAI” if the official closing price for OpenAI’s market capitalization on its first trading day is above the official closing price for SpaceX’s market capitalization on its first trading day.

This market will resolve to the company that completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, if the other company does not complete an IPO within the market timeframe. This market will resolve to the IPOed company as soon as it becomes impossible for the other company to complete an IPO within the market timeframe, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

This market will resolve 50-50 if:
- Neither IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.
- Both SpaceX and OpenAI complete an IPO within this market's timeframe, and their IPO closing market capitalizations are exactly equal.
- Both companies become unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the respective companies’ first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading.

question: Don Lemon sentenced to prison?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Don Lemon is sentenced to any time in jail/prison for any charges related to his arrest on January 30, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will remain open through December 31, 2026 or until Don Lemon is sentenced to time in jail/prison, regardless of any initial sentencing that does not include prison. Any sentencing will qualify, regardless of subsequent appeals or changes to the sentence.

Civil lawsuits, regulatory actions, administrative complaints, investigations without charges, internal employment actions, or public accusations without the filing of criminal charges will not qualify, even if widely reported or described colloquially as “charges.”

If at any point all charges against Don Lemon for all criminal cases are dropped for whatever reason, due to a plea agreement without an admission of guilt, a dismissal, or any other situation in which no judgement is rendered or will be rendered by the court, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government and law enforcement; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 89% implied probability for Don Lemon facing a prison sentence, driven by the complete absence of any criminal charges or indictments against the former CNN anchor. No federal or state prosecutors have pursued cases involving Lemon that could lead to incarceration, with public records showing only civil disputes, such as his ongoing lawsuit against X and Elon Musk over a canceled media deal, which alleges breach of contract but carries no criminal penalties. Recent news reinforces this, including Lemon's active public appearances and lack of court filings signaling trials, underscoring the rumor-based origins of the claim amid social media speculation rather than verifiable legal developments. Upcoming court dates in civil matters are unlikely to shift odds toward conviction risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 83%, reflecting no official action to dismiss misdemeanor summonses against former CNN host Don Lemon stemming from a September 2024 New York City incident where he allegedly ran a red light and struck a cyclist. The summonses—for failure to yield and improper turn—remain active, with no public announcements from Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's office indicating dismissal. Recent reports confirm the cyclist sustained minor injuries, and Lemon has contested the allegations, but standard traffic court processes typically require arraignment or resolution without automatic drops. Absent new developments like a plea deal or prosecutorial discretion, traders anticipate the case proceeds, aligning with historical handling of similar low-level offenses.

question: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Kevin Warsh is confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If Kevin Warsh is formally nominated for the role, and his nomination is then formally withdrawn, or if the Senate rejects the nomination, this market will immediately resolve to “No”.

The Senate must confirm this nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve for this market to resolve to "Yes". Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count toward a "Yes" resolution. Senate confirmation of Warsh as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair by...?

The Senate must confirm this nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve for this market to resolve to "Yes". Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count toward a "Yes" resolution. Senate confirmation of Warsh as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns low implied probability to Kevin Warsh's confirmation as Fed Chair by the market's specified date, primarily due to no official nomination from President-elect Trump's transition team and indications Powell will serve out his term through mid-2026. Warsh, a former Fed governor noted for hawkish inflation stances during 2006-2011, features in media speculation amid broader discussions of monetary policy shifts under a potential second Trump administration. Recent developments include Trump's post-election meetings with economic advisors, but no public endorsement of Warsh. Key upcoming events—cabinet announcements and January 20 inauguration—could catalyze changes, though Senate confirmation remains a hurdle absent a formal pick.

question: Will Thom Tillis vote to confirm Kevin Warsh as Chair of the Federal Reserve?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Senator votes “Yea” on the first final U.S. Senate confirmation vote on the nomination of Kevin Warsh to be Chair of the Federal Reserve. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The vote refers to the first final confirmation vote on the nomination in the full chamber, not including committee votes or procedural motions.

If the nomination passes unanimously or without individual voting (e.g. a voice vote), this market will resolve to “Yes”. If the nomination is rejected unanimously or without individual voting, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no qualifying vote is held by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”. If a formally submitted nomination is withdrawn or returned to the President before reaching a final confirmation vote, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The resolution sources will be official Senate voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will Mexico GDP growth in Q1 2026 be less than 0.0%?
description:
This market will resolve according to Mexico's Y/Y Growth Rate of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Timely Estimate of Quarterly GDP" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for release on April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://en.www.inegi.org.mx/app/saladeprensa/

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a subdued Mexico Q1 2026 GDP growth trajectory, with implied probabilities tightly clustered around 0.5-1.5% (62% combined), signaling consensus on deceleration from recent 2.4% YoY Q3 2024 expansion amid fiscal tightening under President Sheinbaum and softening nearshoring momentum. The razor-thin edge for 0.5-1.0% over 1.0-1.5% (31.5% vs. 30.5%) hinges on Banxico's rate-cut pace—projected to 8% by mid-2025—and U.S. demand risks, including potential tariff hikes post-election. Differentiating catalysts include Q4 2025 manufacturing PMI (key for exports) and remittance trends; sub-1% odds rise if oil stabilizes below $70/barrel, while >1.5% requires robust U.S. GDP above 2%. Upcoming Banxico December meeting will sharpen these market-implied odds.

This market will resolve according to Mexico's Y/Y Growth Rate of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Timely Estimate of Quarterly GDP" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for release on April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://en.www.inegi.org.mx/app/saladeprensa/

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a subdued Mexico Q1 2026 GDP growth trajectory, with implied probabilities tightly clustered around 0.5-1.5% (62% combined), signaling consensus on deceleration from recent 2.4% YoY Q3 2024 expansion amid fiscal tightening under President Sheinbaum and softening nearshoring momentum. The razor-thin edge for 0.5-1.0% over 1.0-1.5% (31.5% vs. 30.5%) hinges on Banxico's rate-cut pace—projected to 8% by mid-2025—and U.S. demand risks, including potential tariff hikes post-election. Differentiating catalysts include Q4 2025 manufacturing PMI (key for exports) and remittance trends; sub-1% odds rise if oil stabilizes below $70/barrel, while >1.5% requires robust U.S. GDP above 2%. Upcoming Banxico December meeting will sharpen these market-implied odds.

question: Will Anthropic or OpenAI IPO first?
description:
This market will resolve to "Anthropic" if Anthropic completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) before OpenAI completes an IPO by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. 

This market will resolve to "OpenAI" if OpenAI completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) before Anthropic completes an IPO by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. 

This market will resolve 50-50 if:
- Neither company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET;
- Both companies complete an IPO on the same calendar date (ET); or
- By December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, both companies become unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders favor Anthropic with a 54% implied probability of IPOing first over OpenAI, primarily due to its straightforward corporate structure backed by Amazon's $8 billion commitment and fewer regulatory entanglements compared to OpenAI's capped-profit model overhaul and FTC probes into Microsoft ties. Recent catalysts include Anthropic's rapid scaling to $18.4 billion valuation amid Claude model releases, signaling IPO readiness, while OpenAI grapples with internal board tensions and a $157 billion private valuation that delays public listing. This razor-thin balance reflects OpenAI's market dominance as a counterweight. Decisive tipping points: S-1 filings, CEO statements at Q1 2025 conferences, or funding rounds explicitly earmarking IPO prep.

question: Will Eurozone GDP growth in Q1 2026 be less than 0.5%?
description:
This market will resolve according to the Eurozone (Euro Area 21) Q1 2026 GDP growth rate over the same quarter of the previous year (% change), based on seasonally adjusted data, in the Eurostat Preliminary Flash Estimate of GDP release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/euro-indicators

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

This market’s resolution source reports GDP growth rates to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced flash GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release of the specified report will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing in subdued Eurozone GDP growth for Q1 2026, with implied probabilities favoring expansion below 0.4% quarter-over-quarter based on aggregated sentiment backed by real capital. This reflects ECB staff projections of 1.2% annual growth in 2026, tempered by Q3 2024's meager 0.2% q/q print amid manufacturing contraction and sticky services inflation near 3.5%. Key drivers include gradual ECB rate cuts to 2.5% by mid-2025 boosting investment, offset by US tariff risks and softening German exports. Traders eye the December 12 ECB meeting and January flash PMIs as pivotal, with official Eurostat data resolving the market in late April 2026.

question: Will Germany GDP growth in Q1 2026 be less than or equal to 0.0%?
description:
This market will resolve according to the German GDP growth rate (% change) on the same quarter a year earlier, price adjusted, as reported in the first release of the Destatis Gross Domestic Product press release for Q1 of 2026, expected to be released on April 30, 2026.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.destatis.de/EN/Themes/Economy/National-Accounts-Domestic-Product/_node.html

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

The resolution source for this market reports GDP growth rates to only one decimal point (e.g. 0.3%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter, or as a part of the next estimate's publication; however, any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release of the specified report will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a leading 28% implied probability to Germany posting 0.1-0.3% GDP growth in Q1 2026, narrowly ahead of 19.7% for 1.3% or higher, reflecting tight competition amid recent economic stabilization after Q3 2024's modest +0.2% quarter-on-quarter expansion that averted recession. Bundesbank and ECB projections eye 1.3-1.6% annual growth for 2026, but quarterly outcomes hinge on differentiating factors like ECB rate cuts boosting domestic demand, fiscal stimulus from the €500 billion debt brake reform fueling infrastructure and defense spending, versus headwinds from weakening exports to China and potential U.S. tariffs under a Trump presidency. Trader consensus tilts cautious on manufacturing PMI weakness, with key Q4 2024 data and January 2026 PMIs poised to sway market-implied odds.

question: Will the Central Bank of Colombia announce a decrease at the April meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the monetary policy rate as a result of the Central Bank of Colombia's April 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Central Bank of Colombia after its April 30, 2026 policy meeting, as listed on the official Central Bank of Colombia calendar: https://www.banrep.gov.co/es/calendario-eventos.

This market may resolve as soon as the Central Bank of Colombia's statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Central Bank of Colombia Decision in April?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the monetary policy rate as a result of the Central Bank of Colombia's April 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Central Bank of Colombia after its April 30, 2026 policy meeting, as listed on the official Central Bank of Colombia calendar: https://www.banrep.gov.co/es/calendario-eventos.

This market may resolve as soon as the Central Bank of Colombia's statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a rate increase by Banco de la República at its April 30 meeting, with an 88.5% implied probability reflecting persistent inflation pressures above the 3% target, as March CPI printed at 7.65% year-over-year despite a slowdown from February's 8.35%. This hawkish sentiment, backed by real capital, stems from sticky core inflation at 7.05%, recent peso depreciation to near 3,900 per dollar amplifying imported price risks, and the bank's March decision to hold rates steady at 12.75% amid hawkish board minutes signaling data dependence. No-change odds at 9.5% capture hold expectations if growth weakens further (Q1 GDP forecasts trimmed), while a 1.4% cut probability underscores low tolerance for easing amid upside inflation risks ahead of key April retail sales data.

question: U.S. anti-cartel ground operation in Mexico by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if U.S. government personnel (military, DEA, CIA, or any other agency) directly participates on the ground in an anti-cartel operation on Mexican soil by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

U.S. personnel must directly participate to qualify. U.S. personnel involved in intelligence, surveillance, logistical, support, or advisory roles will not count. 

Only direct U.S. participation which is either confirmed by the U.S. Government or an overwhelming consensus of reporting will count. For example, previous operations such as the 2014 capture of Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán, in which U.S. forces were rumored to have been embedded with Mexican Marines, would not qualify. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government, however an overwhelming consensus of reporting may also be used.

series.title: U.S. anti-cartel ground operation in Mexico

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for a U.S. anti-cartel ground operation in Mexico, driven by Mexico's staunch defense of sovereignty and lack of official U.S. military planning. President-elect Trump's December 2024 designation of major cartels as foreign terrorist organizations, coupled with threats of force over fentanyl flows and border security, prompted President Sheinbaum's firm rejection of any "invasion." No confirmed troop deployments, congressional authorizations, or joint operations exist, prioritizing diplomatic pressure and USMCA trade leverage. Trump's January 20, 2025, inauguration and early bilateral summits represent key upcoming catalysts that could escalate or de-escalate tensions.

question: Will the South African Reserve Bank decrease the repo rate after the May meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the repo rate resulting from the South African Reserve Bank’s May Monetary Policy Committee meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the South African Reserve Bank after its May 28, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official South African Reserve Bank meeting schedule: https://www.resbank.co.za/en/home/calendar

This market may resolve as soon as the South African Reserve Bank's statement for their May meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the repo rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: South African Reserve Bank Decision in May?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the repo rate resulting from the South African Reserve Bank’s May Monetary Policy Committee meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the South African Reserve Bank after its May 28, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official South African Reserve Bank meeting schedule: https://www.resbank.co.za/en/home/calendar

This market may resolve as soon as the South African Reserve Bank's statement for their May meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the repo rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are split on the South African Reserve Bank's May repo rate decision, with no-change and decrease odds tied at 34% and a hike at 29.5%, reflecting dueling pressures from easing inflation and economic headwinds. Headline CPI held steady at 5.2% YoY in April within the 3-6% target, per StatsSA, bolstering cut bets amid Q1 GDP growth of just 0.1% and improving Eskom power supply. Yet persistent rand weakness near USDZAR 18.30 and post-election coalition risks temper aggressive easing, keeping hike odds viable if inflation surprises upward. Consensus economist forecasts eye 50bps cuts by year-end from the current 8.25% rate, but traders price May 2025 as a coin flip pending Q3 data and Fed path.

question: Will Zelenskyy talk to Putin by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Vladimir Putin talk by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Vladimir Putin, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

Events that occurred prior to the start of this market will not qualify toward its resolution.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Zelenskyy talk to Putin by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Vladimir Putin talk by November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Vladimir Putin, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

Events that occurred prior to the start of this market will not qualify toward its resolution.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus implies low odds for Ukrainian President Zelenskyy holding direct talks with Russian President Putin by the specified date, reflecting Kyiv's precondition of full Russian withdrawal from occupied territories and accountability for war crimes before any dialogue. Zelenskyy reiterated this in recent interviews, rejecting concessions amid stalled frontline advances, while Putin demands recognition of annexed regions like Crimea and Donbas. U.S. election outcomes have fueled speculation of Trump-brokered negotiations, but official statements show no scheduled meetings. Upcoming catalysts include January's U.S. presidential inauguration and potential Saudi-hosted talks, though historical peace efforts like Istanbul 2022 collapsed over irreconcilable terms.

question: U.S. recognizes Machado as leader of Venezuela by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Trump administration announces that the United States officially recognizes Maria Corina Machado as the leader of Venezuela by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Roles that could qualify for leader of Venezuela status include, but are not limited to, "head of state," "president," or other similar roles that give her primary executive authority in the territory of Venezuela.

A qualifying US statement must be direct and unqualified. Conditional, hypothetical, supportive, or implied statements do not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government and Donald Trump, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

A qualifying US statement must be direct and unqualified. Conditional, hypothetical, supportive, or implied statements do not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government and Donald Trump, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US trader consensus heavily favors no recognition of Maria Corina Machado as Venezuela's leader by December 31, driven by President Nicolas Maduro's firm control over military, judiciary, and electoral institutions following his disputed July 28 reelection. Despite US State Department statements affirming opposition evidence that Edmundo Gonzalez—Machado's endorsed candidate—won the popular vote, Washington has imposed targeted sanctions and extended limited oil license relief but stopped short of formal recognition of an opposition government. Recent developments, including Gonzalez's exile in Spain, Maduro's crackdown on dissent, and stalled Caracas talks, reinforce the status quo amid low prospects for institutional shift before year-end. Incoming Trump administration policy remains uncertain but post-January 20.

question: Venezuela election scheduled by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Venezuelan presidential election is scheduled by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To qualify, the election must be scheduled to be held before 2030.

This market is about whether a date for the next Venezuelan presidential election is announced within this market's timeframe. Whether the election is supposed to take place within this market's timeframe or later will have no effect on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the government of Venezuela; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Venezuelan National Electoral Council (CNE), controlled by allies of President Nicolás Maduro, has not announced a new presidential election date after the disputed July 28, 2024, vote, where Maduro was declared winner despite opposition claims of fraud backed by independent tallies showing rival Edmundo González ahead. Maduro's January 10, 2025, inauguration extends his term through 2030, solidifying government control amid suppressed protests and opposition leaders in exile. International skepticism from the US, EU, and OAS has prompted sanctions but no enforcement for a redo. Trader consensus reflects low odds for near-term rescheduling, prioritizing Maduro's institutional dominance over economic woes or diplomatic pressure; watch for CNE statements or regional votes that could signal shifts.

question: Will Trump try to Fire Powell by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump publicly and unequivocally announces that he is removing Jerome Powell as Chair of the Federal Reserve before his term is up, or takes formal action toward doing so, such as issuing a directive or formal request by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Statements which are contingent (e.g. “Powell must step down or lower rates”), statements of intent (e.g.“I am planning to fire Powell”) or other informal statements which are not unequivocal will not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from Donald Trump however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's recent public statements clarifying no intention to remove Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, combined with longstanding legal constraints requiring "cause" for dismissal under the Federal Reserve Act, form the core drivers of traders' 99.4% consensus against any attempt by March 31. Powell's term extends to May 2026, and post-election transition updates show no preparatory actions or nominees signaling imminent replacement, reinforcing Fed independence norms. Historical precedents like Humphrey's Executor affirm removal hurdles, prioritizing stability amid economic policy focus. Realistic shifts could stem from acute inflation spikes or direct policy clashes prompting an abrupt announcement, though traders price these as negligible risks given current dynamics.

question: Grok 5 released by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if xAI's Grok 5 model is made available to the general public by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," Grok 5 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by xAI as being accessible to the general public.

Grok 5 refers to a product explicitly named Grok 5 (e.g. Grok 5.0 would count), or one that is recognized as a successor to Grok 4 similar to the progression from Grok 2 to Grok 3. Products labeled as Grok 4.5 or similar will not count for this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from xAI, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

Grok 2's August debut, featuring improved reasoning rivaling GPT-4o mini, underscores xAI's blistering pace since Grok 1.5V in April, but no official Grok 5 roadmap exists amid compute bottlenecks and talent wars with OpenAI's o1-preview and Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet. Key catalysts include xAI's Memphis expansion announcements and Musk's X posts; historical timeline slips warrant caution for positions.

question: Will Alberta vote for independence in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a referendum on Alberta's independence from Canada is passed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any referendum that establishes Alberta's desire for independence, sets a framework for independence, or establishes independence from Canada will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the government of Alberta, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Alberta vote for independence in 2026?
events.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any referendum that establishes Alberta's desire for independence, sets a framework for independence, or establishes independence from Canada will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the government of Alberta, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alberta traders heavily favor "No" at 92.2% implied probability on a 2026 independence vote, driven by the absence of any official referendum legislation or timeline from the provincial government. Premier Danielle Smith, leader of the United Conservative Party, explicitly rejected separatism in recent statements, emphasizing Alberta sovereignty within Canada amid federal tensions over energy policy and equalization payments. Polls consistently show support below 30%, with most residents opposing separation due to economic risks tied to oil exports and trade. Fringe groups like the Alberta Prosperity Project push petitions, but lack mainstream backing or legislative traction, reinforcing trader consensus absent major catalysts like a ballot commitment.

question: Will the RSF capture El Obeid by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) capture El Obeid (https://maps.app.goo.gl/NQaTN51errVH9KyN6) by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The city will be considered captured if the Rapid Support forces have established military control over the "El-Obeid Great Mosque" (مسجد الأبيّض الكبير: https://maps.app.goo.gl/LyxD1PPGux7CYSdYA) by the resolution date. 

If the RSF comes into control of the city as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a “Yes” resolution. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that grants the RSF de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once the RSF captures the specified place, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting, including video and photo evidence that the RSF has captured the specified point.

events.title: Will the RSF capture El Obeid by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) capture El Obeid (https://maps.app.goo.gl/NQaTN51errVH9KyN6) by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The city will be considered captured if the Rapid Support forces have established military control over the "El-Obeid Great Mosque" (مسجد الأبيّض الكبير: https://maps.app.goo.gl/LyxD1PPGux7CYSdYA) by the resolution date. 

If the RSF comes into control of the city as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a “Yes” resolution. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that grants the RSF de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once the RSF captures the specified place, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting, including video and photo evidence that the RSF has captured the specified point.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong consensus against the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) capturing El Obeid by March 31 stems from stalled RSF offensives in North Kordofan, where Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) maintain firm control of the strategic city despite months of encirclement and clashes. Recent reports confirm no breakthroughs in the past week, with SAF reinforcements and airstrikes blunting RSF probes, while RSF forces appear overstretched from prior Darfur gains. With days remaining, the tight timeline reinforces 96% "No" odds, reflecting wisdom-of-crowds assessment of logistical hurdles. Realistic shifts could arise from an unforeseen RSF surge or SAF internal disruptions, though evidence points to continued stalemate.

question: Will Elon Musk be 3rd richest person on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve according to the name ranked #3 on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index on March 31, 2026, 5:30 PM ET.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Bloomberg Billionaires Index (https://www.bloomberg.com/billionaires/). If the data for the specified date is not released by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the Forbes Real-Time Billionaires List will be used (https://www.forbes.com/real-time-billionaires/#6aa3f0213d78). If neither source provides the specified date's data by April 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve according to the latest data point available on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Sergey Brin at 86% implied probability to claim the third-richest spot on March 31, driven by Alphabet's surging stock amid AI breakthroughs and strong quarterly earnings that have ballooned his Google founder stake. Recent developments, including Google's Gemini model advancements and ad revenue beats, have propelled Brin past challengers, with Larry Page close behind at 3% due to identical holdings but slightly lower positioning. Jeff Bezos holds steady at 7% on Amazon's e-commerce dominance, while Elon Musk (1.7%) and Mark Zuckerberg (1.8%) face volatility from Tesla/SpaceX and Meta's capex risks. Bloomberg Billionaires Index updates will resolve, underscoring tech wealth concentration amid market rotations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Larry Page holds a slim edge over Warren Buffett in trader consensus for second-richest on December 31, driven by Alphabet's relentless AI momentum and Google Cloud growth outpacing Berkshire Hathaway's value-oriented stability amid market volatility. Bernard Arnault trails closely at 30%, buoyed by LVMH's luxury rebound and holiday spending surge, while Mark Zuckerberg's Meta stock rally has narrowed the gap but faces year-end ad revenue risks. Competitive dynamics hinge on final-week tech selloffs or Fed signals boosting Buffett, with wildcards like Nvidia's Jensen Huang riding AI hype and Elon Musk's Tesla distractions capping his odds despite top-spot dominance; resolution awaits Bloomberg or Forbes' December 31 snapshot.

question: Will the RSF capture Kadugli by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) capture Kadugli (https://maps.app.goo.gl/YY4PX4vESfgZtETQ6) by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The city will be considered captured if the Rapid Support forces have established military control over the "Police Hospital" (مستشفى الشرطة: https://maps.app.goo.gl/nQ1tkNERZs1J4Uw2A) by the resolution date. 

If the RSF comes into control of the city as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a “Yes” resolution. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that grants the RSF de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once the RSF captures the specified place, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting, including video and photo evidence that the RSF has captured the specified point.

events.title: Will the RSF capture Kadugli by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) capture Kadugli (https://maps.app.goo.gl/YY4PX4vESfgZtETQ6) by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The city will be considered captured if the Rapid Support forces have established military control over the "Police Hospital" (مستشفى الشرطة: https://maps.app.goo.gl/nQ1tkNERZs1J4Uw2A) by the resolution date. 

If the RSF comes into control of the city as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a “Yes” resolution. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that grants the RSF de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.
