The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting, including video and photo evidence that the RSF has captured the specified point.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no RSF capture of Kadugli by March 31, reflecting SAF's firm hold on the South Kordofan capital amid ongoing clashes. Recent reports confirm intense RSF shelling and advances in nearby areas like Dilling, but SAF reinforcements and local tribal militias have repelled assaults on the city center, with no verified breakthroughs as of late March. Logistical challenges for RSF in the rugged terrain, combined with just days remaining before the deadline, underpin the 91.3% implied probability of no capture. Markets await any last-minute developments, though historical patterns in Sudan's civil war show such urban strongholds rarely fall swiftly.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the third-richest spot on December 31 hinges on a nail-biting tech stock showdown, with Mark Zuckerberg's 35% implied probability edging Sergey Brin (33.5%) and Jensen Huang (33%) amid Nvidia's explosive AI-driven rally and Meta's ad revenue surge. Bloomberg's real-time index shows Zuckerberg at fourth (~$205B), Huang climbing to sixth (~$140B) on Nvidia shares up 180% YTD, Brin/Page holding strong via Alphabet's cloud gains, and Ellison trailing slightly on Oracle's steady enterprise growth. Differentiators include Huang's leveraged Nvidia stake amid Blackwell chip hype versus Zuckerberg's diversified Meta bets and Alphabet duo's search dominance; with weeks left, trader consensus anticipates year-end volatility from Fed signals and AI fervor over any billionaire shakeups.

question: Will the US deport illegal migrants to Argentina by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any illegal alien who is not a citizen of Argentina in the custody of U.S. law enforcement or correctional authorities, is transferred to Argentina for the purpose of incarceration or deportation by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To qualify, an illegal alien must be transferred to Argentina, and physically enter the terrestrial territory. Entering the maritime territory or airspace will not qualify.

Any transfer meeting these criteria will count, even if its implementation is immediately halted or reversed by judicial or other actions.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. Government, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 97.8% implied probability for US deportations of illegal migrants to Argentina by March 31, driven by the absence of any official bilateral agreement, scheduled flights, or diplomatic progress between Washington and Buenos Aires. Recent US removal operations have targeted home countries and third-party destinations like El Salvador for Venezuelans, with over 300 deported there on March 16 amid President Trump's mass deportation push, but none to Argentina despite President Milei's public overtures on receiving certain criminals. Logistical hurdles, including distance and capacity, compound the tight timeline, leaving little room for action. A sudden deal or emergency flight remains a slim possibility, though traders see negligible risk.

question: Will Russia enter Dobropillia by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Dobropillia by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

This market refers to the city of Dobropillia (48.47, 37.08) which borders Sviatohorivka and Hannivka. Other Ukrainian towns/cities named Dobropillia will not be considered for resolution.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of Dobropillia is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified advances in Donetsk Oblast's Pokrovsk sector, capturing villages like Shevchenko and advancing toward Dobropillia, a key logistical hub 10km southwest of Pokrovsk, as Ukrainian military reports indicate heavy fighting and partial encirclement risks. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution, pricing low odds for entry by the deadline amid Ukraine's fortified defenses bolstered by recent Western aid deliveries, including artillery shells. Winter weather could slow mechanized assaults, while upcoming events like potential U.S. policy shifts post-election and NATO winter aid packages may influence Ukrainian reinforcement capabilities, underscoring the fluid frontline dynamics driving market probabilities.

question: Another US bank failure by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any US bank fails between this market's creation and the listed date 11:59 PM ET (according to the FDIC's "Failed Bank List"). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes", the bank's closing date as listed by the FDIC must be within this market's above-specified timeframe. If there is a potential bank failure within this market's timeframe and the FDIC "Failed Bank List" has not been updated yet, this market may remain open to allow for the list to be updated.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), specifically the "Failed Bank List" available here: https://www.fdic.gov/resources/resolutions/bank-failures/failed-bank-list/; however, other official statements from the FDIC and government entities will suffice.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), specifically the "Failed Bank List" available here: https://www.fdic.gov/resources/resolutions/bank-failures/failed-bank-list/; however, other official statements from the FDIC and government entities will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Post-2023 regulatory reforms, including enhanced capital requirements and stress testing, have significantly bolstered US bank resilience, anchoring the 92% market-implied probability of no failure by March 31. Trader consensus reflects no closures since Republic First National Bank's FDIC takeover in April 2024, with the number of problem banks dropping to 39 per latest FDIC data amid robust Q4 earnings and steady deposit inflows. Broad economic strength—2.8% GDP growth and sub-4% unemployment—curbs loan defaults. However, concentrated commercial real estate exposure in regional lenders could challenge this if office vacancies surge or a recession triggers uninsured deposit runs, though current stress test results suggest ample buffers.

question: UNCW Seahawks vs. Charleston Cougars
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for February 7 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the UNCW Seahawks win, the market will resolve to "UNCW Seahawks".

If the Charleston Cougars win, the market will resolve to "Charleston Cougars".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: UNCW Seahawks vs. Charleston Cougars
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between UNCW Seahawks and Charleston Cougars on February 7 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

question: Will anyone be jailed over Epstein disclosures?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, any individual serves any time in a federal, state, or local U.S. jail or prison, and the cause of that incarceration is attributed to information contained in files related to Jeffrey Epstein released by the federal government on or after December 19, 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A qualifying incarceration must be caused by information included in Epstein-related files released on or after December 19, 2025. The cause of incarceration may be established through official charging documents, court rulings, sentencing statements, or through a clear consensus of credible reporting attributing the incarceration to information contained in those released files. Incarceration driven by information that was publicly known before December 19, 2025, or by reasons unrelated to the content of the released Epstein-related files, will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be official court records or government statements, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favoring "No" at 88.5% stems from the absence of new criminal charges or arrests since the January 2024 unsealing of Epstein-related court documents from the Giuffre-Maxwell civil case. These files, hyped as a potential "client list," largely restated prior public allegations without fresh prosecutable evidence, as confirmed by DOJ reviews. Ghislaine Maxwell's 2022 conviction predates the disclosures and involved her direct role, not new revelations. No federal probes have advanced against named figures like Bill Clinton or Prince Andrew, hampered by statutes of limitations and Epstein's 2019 death. Recent FBI clarifications dismissed further actionable leads, solidifying low expectations for jailings amid evidentiary challenges.

question: Will anyone be charged over Epstein disclosures?
description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying charge or indictment must be caused by information included in Epstein-related files released on or after December 19, 2025. Charges or indictments driven by information that was publicly known before December 19, 2025, or by reasons unrelated to the content of the released Epstein-related files, will not qualify.

question: Will Ex-Prince Andrew Testify to Congress about Epstein?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual provides testimony about Jeffrey Epstein before either chamber of the United States Congress (House of Representatives or Senate), or any of their official committees or subcommittees, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Qualifying testimony may be either live or recorded, in-person or remote, and closed or open to the public.

A primary focus of the testimony must be information related to Jeffrey Epstein, including but not limited to the listed individuals’ relationship with Epstein, their appearance in Epstein-related files released by the federal government, their involvement in previous Epstein investigations or criminal proceedings, or their general knowledge of Epstein and his life. Limited references to Epstein in an otherwise unrelated congressional testimony will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair by May 1?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Kevin Warsh is confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve by May 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If Kevin Warsh is formally nominated for the role, and his nomination is then formally withdrawn, or if the Senate rejects the nomination, this market will immediately resolve to “No”.

The Senate must confirm this nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve for this market to resolve to "Yes". Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count toward a "Yes" resolution. Senate confirmation of Warsh as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns low implied probability to Kevin Warsh's confirmation as Fed Chair by the market's specified date, primarily due to no official nomination from President-elect Trump's transition team and indications Powell will serve out his term through mid-2026. Warsh, a former Fed governor noted for hawkish inflation stances during 2006-2011, features in media speculation amid broader discussions of monetary policy shifts under a potential second Trump administration. Recent developments include Trump's post-election meetings with economic advisors, but no public endorsement of Warsh. Key upcoming events—cabinet announcements and January 20 inauguration—could catalyze changes, though Senate confirmation remains a hurdle absent a formal pick.

question: Will Diego Lopes fight Alexander Volkanovski next?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Alexander Volkanovski is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Alexander Volkanovski is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Volkanovski is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors Movsar Evloev (66.5%) as Alexander Volkanovski's next featherweight opponent, propelled by Evloev's perfect 18-0 record and recent unanimous decision win over Aljamain Sterling at UFC 307, solidifying his #1 contender status. Volkanovski, sidelined since his February 2024 knockout loss to Ilia Topuria and a UFC 303 withdrawal due to concussion protocols, is now cleared and training, with UFC executives hinting at a title eliminator against Evloev. Jean Silva's 36.5% share stems from his blistering five-fight KO streak and bold callout after UFC 308, though his prospect status lags behind Evloev. Arnold Allen (12.8%) persists via his veteran pedigree, but inactivity dims his momentum amid the crowded division.
